title: Google’s Gemini delay exposes a deeper problem: employee morale
excerpt: Google is losing a race it once led, and the problem may be its own people. Six current and former DeepMind employees told Axios that low morale is slowing the lab’s model releases, Axios reported. The immediate symptom is Gemini 3.5 Pro. Google’s most powerful model is months behind, according to Bloomberg. Google did ship […]
content_html:
Google is losing a race it once led, and the problem may be its own people. Six current and former DeepMind employees told Axios that low morale is slowing the lab’s model releases, Axios reported.

The immediate symptom is Gemini 3.5 Pro. Google’s most powerful model is months behind, according to Bloomberg. Google did ship a batch of smaller, cheaper models this week, to mixed reviews. Rivals pounced. “Gemini who?” Meta’s Alexandr Wang posted on X.

The timing stings because the money is flowing out. Google beat earnings on Wednesday, but its free cash flow turned negative, largely on AI spending. It is on track to spend $190bn this year.

Cloud revenue grew 82%, yet search came in light, sharpening the question of whether the capex will pay off.

A talent exodus

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The deeper issue is who is leaving. The AI talent wars have hit Google hardest, and it has lost big names. Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer went to OpenAI. Nobel chemistry laureate John Jumper left for Anthropic. “We’re behind,” one employee told Axios, of the company’s models against rivals.

One flashpoint keeps surfacing: an April deal letting the Pentagon use Google’s technology. Several employees say they resigned over it, and sources said it comes up often in exit interviews. For those opposed, one called it a “constant battle” that has led to “emotional burnout.”

Alex Turner, a former DeepMind scientist who quit over the military work, said chief executive Demis Hassabis lacks the internal presence that Sam Altman and Dario Amodei have with their staff.

Google pushes back

Google rejects the picture. It disputes that morale is denting its models, or that people are leaving in droves over the Pentagon deal. Attrition among AI staff, it says, is lower than a year ago, and more than 90% of those offered AI roles accept.

“We’re feeling good about this week’s Flash launches, our roadmap and the incredible demand,” a spokesperson said. Its chief executive Hassabis also published an AI safety framework last week.

There is truth on both sides. Leads in AI are fickle, and Google has been leapfrogged before, by Anthropic’s Mythos, and may be again by OpenAI next. The cheaper models it just shipped answer a real market worry about cost.

But the bottom line is stark.

Google can afford to be overtaken on a leaderboard. It cannot afford to lose its best people, or its investors’ patience.

sentences.zh: 谷歌反击

sentences.zh: 双方都有道理。

sentences.en: Leads in AI are fickle, and Google has been leapfrogged before, by Anthropic’s Mythos, and may be again by OpenAI next.
sentences.zh: 人工智能领域的领先地位是变化无常的，谷歌之前曾被 Anthropic 的 Mythos 超越，接下来可能会再次被 OpenAI 超越。

sentences.en: The cheaper models it just shipped answer a real market worry about cost.
sentences.zh: 它刚刚推出的更便宜的型号解决了市场对成本的真正担忧。

sentences.zh: 但底线是严峻的。

blocks.html_en: Google can afford to be overtaken on a leaderboard. It cannot afford to lose its best people, or its investors’ patience.

sentences.en: Google can afford to be overtaken on a leaderboard.
sentences.zh: 谷歌有能力在排行榜上被超越。

sentences.en: It cannot afford to lose its best people, or its investors’ patience.
sentences.zh: 它不能失去最优秀的人才，也不能失去投资者的耐心。

content_html_zh:
谷歌正在输掉一场它曾经领先的比赛，问题可能出在它自己的员工身上。 据 Axios 报道，六名现任和前任 DeepMind 员工告诉 Axios，士气低落正在减慢实验室模型的发布速度。

最直接的症状是 Gemini 3.5 Pro。 据彭博社报道，谷歌最强大的模型已经落后了几个月。 谷歌本周确实推出了一批更小、更便宜的型号，但评价褒贬不一。 竞争对手猛扑过来。 “双子座是谁？” Meta 的 Alexandr Wang 在 X 上发帖。

时机很关键，因为资金正在外流。 谷歌周三的财报超出了预期，但其自由现金流转为负值，这主要归功于人工智能支出。 今年预计支出 1900 亿美元。

云收入增长了 82%，但搜索业务却开始崭露头角，加剧了资本支出能否获得回报的问题。

人才外流

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

更深层次的问题是谁离开。 人工智能人才大战对谷歌的打击最为严重，它失去了一些知名人士。 Gemini 联合领导者 Noam Shazeer 加入了 OpenAI。 诺贝尔化学奖得主约翰·詹珀 (John Jumper) 前往 Anthropic。 “我们落后了，”一名员工在谈到该公司针对竞争对手的模式时告诉 Axios。

一个热点不断浮出水面：四月份的一项协议允许五角大楼使用谷歌的技术。 几名员工表示，他们因此辞职，消息人士称，这在离职面谈中经常出现。 对于那些反对者来说，有人称这是一场“持续的战斗”，导致了“情感倦怠”。

因军事工作而辞职的前 DeepMind 科学家亚历克斯·特纳 (Alex Turner) 表示，首席执行官德米斯·哈萨比斯 (Demis Hassabis) 缺乏萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 和达里奥·阿莫迪 (Dario Amodei) 及其员工那样的内部影响力。

谷歌反击

谷歌拒绝了这张图片。 它质疑士气正在削弱其模型，或者人们因五角大楼的交易而成群结队地离开。 报告称，人工智能员工的流失率低于一年前，并且超过 90% 的人工智能职位接受了。

“我们对本周的 Flash 发布、我们的路线图和令人难以置信的需求感到满意，”一位发言人表示。 其首席执行官哈萨比斯上周还发布了人工智能安全框架。

双方都有道理。 人工智能领域的领先地位是变化无常的，谷歌之前曾被 Anthropic 的 Mythos 超越，接下来可能会再次被 OpenAI 超越。 它刚刚推出的更便宜的型号解决了市场对成本的真正担忧。

但底线是严峻的。

谷歌有能力在排行榜上被超越。 它不能失去最优秀的人才，也不能失去投资者的耐心。
